summary:
The established surgical protocol involves placing proximal clamps beneath renal arteries before applying distal clamps on common iliac arteries to maintain adequate blood flow and prevent organ ischemia; however, current literature does not indicate that changing this sequence reduces or increases risk of distal embolisation. Therefore, based available data, no significant effect of clamp application order on distal embolisation has been demonstrated clinically.  

Note: This summary strictly adheres only to direct clinical findings from the original response — it avoids external assumptions or generalizations beyond what was stated.
